summary: This brief blog post announces a new Bloomberg column written in collaboration with ageism activist Ashton Applewhite about the dangers facing older people during the COVID-19 pandemic. The column argues that using biased pandemic data to allocate resources could perpetuate high mortality rates among the elderly. O'Neil connects her expertise in algorithmic bias with Applewhite's advocacy work to highlight how data-driven decisions during crises can discriminate against vulnerable populations.
key_points: Pandemic data used for resource allocation may contain biases that harm older people
Collaboration between data science and activism perspectives can illuminate overlooked harms
Crisis-time algorithmic decisions require scrutiny for age-based discrimination
money_quotes: Using biased data to allocate resources could perpetuate a high mortality rate.
key_insight: Data-driven pandemic responses risk encoding ageism into life-or-death resource allocation decisions.
